column1: Tangency Portfolio (long-only, max Sharpe ratio, max_weight=0.4)

The tangency portfolio maximizes the Sharpe ratio (E[r] - rf)/sigma subject to the same long-only and weight-cap constraints as the Optimal sheet. Where the Optimal sheet maximizes utility for a given A, this sheet maximizes risk-adjusted return — a different objective. On a chart, this is the portfolio where the CML touches the frontier.
Expected result on the canonical dataset: Sharpe ~ 0.79 (matches Python tangency).
If the optimum looks degenerate (single-asset concentration), tighten Convergence to 1e-9.
